ddls:
CREATE TABLE "json_operations" (
  "operation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each JSON operation */,
  "operator" TEXT /* The operator used in the JSON operation */,
  "right_operand_type" TEXT /* The data type of the right operand in the operation */,
  "description" TEXT /* A detailed description of the JSON operation */,
  "example" TEXT /* An example of how the JSON operation is used */,
  "example_result" TEXT /* The result of the example JSON operation */,
  "operation_type" TEXT /* The type of JSON operation (e.g., extract, modify, delete) */,
  "is_deprecated" INTEGER /* Indicates if the operation is deprecated */,
  "performance_impact" TEXT /* The impact on system performance (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("operation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "json_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each JSON data entry */,
  "data_json" TEXT /* The JSON data itself, stored in a JSONB format for efficient querying */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* The type of JSON data (e.g., object, array) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the JSON data was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the JSON data was last updated */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the JSON data */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last updated the JSON data */,
  "version" INTEGER /* Version number of the JSON data */,
  "locked" INTEGER /* Indicates if the data is locked for editing */,
  "locked_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who locked the data */,
  "locked_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the data was locked */,
  "tags" TEXT /* Tags to categorize the data */,
  "source_system" TEXT /* The source system where the data originated */,
  "data_size" INTEGER /* Size of the JSON data in bytes */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_json_data_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_json_data_updated_by FOREIGN KEY ("updated_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_json_data_locked_by FOREIGN KEY ("locked_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., data scientist, developer, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* List of permissions the user has */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user (e.g., active, suspended) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
